certificate_of_origin_no: Find the certificate number by labels "Certificate of Origin No" / "Certificate No" / "Register No" / "REG. No" / "REGISTER No" / "No MM" (composite local-format prefix + digits). Keep as one continuous string preserving internal segments and prefixes (e.g., "No MM 1130273", "Certificate No. C266048588250019", "Certificate No.20250469548110IL05"). Do NOT drop letter prefixes (MM, C). Ignore the barcode in the upper right corner — it contains the same number in another encoding.
certificate_of_origin_date: Extract from the certifying authority block ("Place and date, signature and stamp of certifying authority"). Formats vary: "[City], [Country], [Date]" (e.g., "Qingdao, China, APR 16,2025") or "[Country] [DD/MM/YYYY]" (e.g., "TURKIYE 26/03/2025"). Extract only the date, dropping the place. Supported date formats: "APR 16,2025" / "FEB. 06, 2026" / "26/03/2025" / DD.MM.YYYY. If multiple dates exist (exporter declaration date in box 11 vs. certification date in box 12), prioritize the certification date (box 12).
invoice_no: Extract invoice number(s) from box 10 "Number and date of invoices" or from text inside box 7 description (e.g., "INVOICE: A012025000000055/ 26.03.2025"). If multiple, join with "; ". Add to invoice_no.
invoice_date: Next to invoice number(s), extract invoice date(s). If multiple, join with "; ". Keep format as in document and add to invoice_date.
country_of_origin: Sources in priority order: (1) explicit field "Country of Origin" / "Mense Ulkesi" / "Pays d'origine" (typically in box 3); (2) the document title "CERTIFICATE OF ORIGIN OF [COUNTRY]" — the origin is embedded in the title itself; (3) the producer declaration in box 11 "the above goods were produced in [COUNTRY]". Do NOT confuse with "Country of destination" (typically in box 4) — that is the recipient country, not origin. Add to country_of_origin.
exporter: Take from box 1. Labels: "Exporter" / "Consignor" / "Sender" / "Expediteur" / "Ihracatci" / similar in the local language. Keep the full legal name with address and VAT/tax-ID if present (e.g., "AKERKO SU URUNLERI ... VAT: CARSIBASI MM-023 030 23 43"). Join multi-line address into one line. Add to exporter.
importer: Take from box 2. Labels: "Consignee" / "Destinataire" / "Alici" / similar. Keep the full name and address in one line. Add to importer.
destination: Sources in priority order: (1) "Destination port" (typically box 4); (2) "Country / region of destination" (box 4); (3) the route in box 3 "Means of transport and route" — extract the destination from "FROM ... TO [DEST] BY ..."; (4) if no explicit field, derive country from the consignee address (box 2). Add to destination.
vessel: In CO, box 3 ("Means of transport and route") typically lists only the TRANSPORT MODE ("BY SEA" / "BY TRUCK" / "BY TRAIN" / "BY AIR") and the ROUTE ("FROM ... TO ...") — vessel name is usually NOT present. Leave empty unless an explicit vessel name appears. Add to vessel.
container_no: Extract container number(s) from the REMARKS section INSIDE box 7 (description of goods) — typically after a separator line like "*** *** ***" or after a "REMARKS:" label. Format: "CONTAINER NO. : [number]" or "CONTAINER:[number]". Number format: 4 letters + 7 digits. If multiple, join with "; ". Add to container_no.
veterinary_seal: Extract only explicit veterinary/health seal numbers ("Veterinary seal" / "Health seal"). Do not confuse with linear seal/Seal No. CO usually does not contain this field. If none, leave empty. Add to veterinary_seal.
linear_seal: If the certificate lists seal numbers ("Seal No." / "Seal Number") for containers, extract them. CO usually does not contain this field. If multiple, join with "; ". Add to linear_seal.
HS_code: In CO, HS code is often given as a CHAPTER (2 digits with dot, e.g., "03.03", "20.08") or HEADING (4 digits with dot), NOT as a full 10-digit code. This is normal — do not pad to 10 digits. May also be entirely ABSENT. If multiple per product lines, collect unique and join with "; ". Keep format as printed. Add to HS_code.
products[*].name_product: Take the commercial name from the goods description in box 7. If multi-line, join into one line. Put Latin species in latin_name. Add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Latin species name in binomial format (Genus species), in parentheses. Multiple parenthesized blocks may appear (e.g., for processed products like seaweed salad with both a brand name and a Latin name in separate parentheses) — filter strictly by binomial pattern (two uppercase words). Add to products[i].latin_name.
products[*].size_product: If size/grade is shown, extract. May be ABSENT (e.g., for processed products). Add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: If an accounting/pricing unit is explicitly shown, extract. CO usually does not contain pricing — if absent, leave empty.
products[*].packages: Extract number of packages. The number may be DUPLICATED in words and digits (e.g., "FORTY (40) BAGS", "TWO THOUSAND FIVE HUNDRED (2500) CTNS") — extract only the numeric part with type: "40 BAGS", "2500 CTNS". Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].price_per_unit: If unit price is shown in CO (rare), extract. If no price, leave empty.
products[*].total_price: If line total is shown in CO (rare), extract. If no amount, leave empty (do not compute).
products[*].net_weight: Net weight in CO may be in three places: (1) inside the description in box 7 ("TOTAL N.W.: X KGS" / "NET: X KG"); (2) in column 9 "Quantity" with N.W. suffix ("X KGS N.W."); (3) a separate column (rare). Search by suffixes "N.W." / "Net" / "NET". Do not confuse with total quantity without N.W./G.W. suffix — that may be gross. Add to products[i].net_weight.
products[*].gross_weight: Extract gross weight from column 9 ("X KGS G.W.") or as a value without suffix in the Quantity column. If a value is given without an N.W./G.W. suffix, it is GROSS by default. Number format may use European thousands/decimal separators (e.g., "21.088,35" — dot for thousands, comma for decimal). Add to products[i].gross_weight.
